claim: 25% of patients with melanoma and an objective response to PD-1 blockade will experience a progression in their melanoma.

result.claim: 25% of patients with melanoma and an objective response to PD-1 blockade will experience a progression in their melanoma.
result.reasoning: The claim states that 25% of patients with melanoma and an objective response to PD-1 blockade will experience a progression. However, evidence from a cohort study (Paper ID: 35366571) with a sample size of 108 patients, states that "Thirty to forty percent of initial responders to PD-1-based immunotherapy with melanoma also progress." This directly contradicts the 25% figure in the claim.

evidence.text: The ORR was 53% in 19 efficacy evaluable melanoma patients.
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: Nivolumab shown convincing statistically significant results in the first-line treatment of metastatic melanoma.
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: The melanoma patients were treated with checkpoint inhibitors.
evidence.source: Targeted deletion of CD244 on monocytes promotes differentiation into anti-tumorigenic macrophages and potentiates PD-L1 blockade in melanoma.

evidence.text: Immunotherapy with immune-checkpoint molecule inhibitors increases the overall survival of melanoma patients.
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: Combining a selective BRAF inhibitor and a MEK inhibitor has been shown to improve the response rate and progression-free survival in patients with advanced melanoma.
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: In 19 efficacy evaluable melanoma patients, the disease control rate was 74%.
evidence.source: Melanoma and immunotherapy bridge 2015 : Naples, Italy. 1-5 December 2015.

evidence.text: Science published "Genomic correlates of response to CTLA-4 blockade in metastatic melanoma".

evidence.text: The study evaluated the outcome of second-line targeted therapy (TT) after progression on PD-1-based immune checkpoint inhibition (ICI) in BRAFV600-mutated melanoma.
evidence.source: MAPKinase inhibition after failure of immune checkpoint blockade in patients with advanced melanoma - An evaluation of the multicenter prospective skin cancer registry ADOREG.
